title: My Anxiety Is Information, Not Identity
subtitle: An affirmation for separating the signal from the self
excerpt: A short affirmation for when anxiety has become a defining label rather than a signal. The statement names what anxiety is and what it is not.

practice.whenToUse: Use when anxiety has been running long enough that it has started to feel like who you are rather than something you experience.

content.text: A one-minute affirmation for separating the signal from the self. When anxiety runs consistently enough, it starts to feel like a permanent feature rather than a response. The statement corrects that frame.
New to affirmations as a practice? Read
My anxiety is information, not identity. It tells me something. It is not who I am.
Say it when the anxiety is present and feels permanent. Say it three times. Say it again if the sense of it being you rather than something you are having has settled back in.
